name: PORTAL VEIN
definition: waveform pulsatility with > ²/³ change from peak velocity to minimum velocity waveform below the baseline accumulation of lymph around the portal tract ill-defined hypodensity at porta hepatis / hypodensity parallel to portal vein

demographics.Location: predominantly in left lobe = nondependent position when patient supine
demographics.Associated with: portal venous hypertension, congenital abnormalities, umbilical vein

dominant_finding: tubular branching areas of decreased attenuation (similar to air) in periphery 2 cm deep
distribution: predominantly in left lobe = nondependent position when patient supine
discriminating_features: decreased S wave in hepatic venous Doppler
tall a and v waves in hepatic venous Doppler
cavernous transformation = development of collateral vessels in / around occluded
